plainEnglish: Give users a way to skip past content repeated on every page—like the header and nav—straight to the main content. A 'skip to main content' link or proper landmark regions (<main>, <nav>) lets keyboard and screen reader users avoid tabbing through the same menu on every page.
whyItMatters: Keyboard and screen reader users otherwise have to tab through dozens of repeated navigation links on every single page before reaching the content.
quickCheck: Tab once from the top of the page and confirm a 'Skip to main content' link appears, or verify there are <main>/<nav> landmarks (axe's bypass rule).
commonMistakes: No skip link and no landmark regions, so users must tab through the full nav on every page.
A skip link permanently hidden with display:none instead of visually-hidden-until-focused.
A skip link whose target has no id or isn't focusable, so it doesn't actually move focus.
Wrapping everything in <div>s instead of using <main>, <nav>, and <header> landmarks.

codeExamples.label: Skip link plus a main landmark
codeExamples.code:
Skip to main content
{/* repeated navigation */}
{/* page content */}

codeExamples.label: No way to bypass the repeated nav
codeExamples.code:
{/* repeated navigation, no skip mechanism */}
{/* page content */}
